The displayed name of this project. If l: The displayed name of this project. If left empty, the root directory name will be displayed.
What to do when using the 'close active: What to do when using the 'close active item' action with no tabs.
What to do when the last window is close: What to do when the last window is closed.
Use native OS dialogs for 'Open' and 'Sa: Use native OS dialogs for 'Open' and 'Save As'.
Use native OS dialogs for confirmations.
Show CPU, memory, and GPU usage in the s: Show CPU, memory, and GPU usage in the status bar.
Hide the values of variables in private: Hide the values of variables in private files.
Globs to match against file paths to det: Globs to match against file paths to determine if a file is private.
When opening Zed, avoid Restricted Mode: When opening Dokkaebi, avoid Restricted Mode by auto-trusting all projects, enabling use of all features without having to give permission to each new project.
Whether or not to restore unsaved buffer: Whether or not to restore unsaved buffers on restart.
What to restore from the previous sessio: What to restore from the previous session when opening Dokkaebi.
Which settings should be activated only: Which settings should be activated only in Preview build of Dokkaebi.
Any number of settings profiles that are: Any number of settings profiles that are temporarily applied on top of your existing user settings.
Send debug information like crash report: Send debug information like crash reports.
Send anonymized usage data like what lan: Send anonymized usage data like what languages you're using Dokkaebi with.
Checks for the latest updates when the a: Checks for the latest updates when the app starts.
Skip Focus For Active In Search: Skip Focus For Active In Search
Enable Preview Multibuffer From Code Nav: Enable Preview Multibuffer From Code Navigation
Enable Preview File From Code Navigation: Enable Preview File From Code Navigation
Enable Keep Preview On Code Navigation: Enable Keep Preview On Code Navigation
Show the notepad panel button in the sta: Show the notepad panel button in the status bar.
Where to dock the notepad panel.
Restore notepad content on app startup.
Controls line number display in the edit: Controls line number display in the editor's gutter. "disabled" shows absolute line numbers, "enabled" shows relative line numbers for each absolute line, and "wrapped" shows relative line numbers for every line, absolute or wrapped.
How to highlight the current line in the: How to highlight the current line in the minimap.
Show quick action buttons (e.g., search,: Show quick action buttons (e.g., search, selection, editor controls, etc.).
When enabled, the :substitute command re: When enabled, the :substitute command replaces all matches in a line by default. The 'g' flag then toggles this behavior.
Cursor shape for insert mode. Inherit us: Cursor shape for insert mode. Inherit uses the editor's cursor shape.
The delay in milliseconds to show inline: The delay in milliseconds to show inline diagnostics after the last diagnostic update.
The amount of padding between the end of: The amount of padding between the end of the source line and the start of the inline diagnostic.
The minimum column at which to display i: The minimum column at which to display inline diagnostics.
Determines how much space the file finde: Determines how much space the file finder can take up in relation to the available window width.
Whether the file finder should skip focu: Whether the file finder should skip focus for the active file in search results.
Files or globs of files that will be exc: Files or globs of files that will be excluded by Dokkaebi entirely. They will be skipped during file scans, file searches, and not be displayed in the project file tree. Takes precedence over "file_scan_inclusions".
Files or globs of files that will be inc: Files or globs of files that will be included by Dokkaebi, even when ignored by git. This is useful for files that are not tracked by git, but are still important to your project. Note that globs that are overly broad can slow down Dokkaebi's file scanning. "**/.git" is always included.
Control when to show the active encoding: Control when to show the active encoding in the status bar.
Show banners announcing new features in: Show banners announcing new features in the titlebar.
Show the tab bar buttons (New, Split Pan: Show the tab bar buttons (New, Split Pane, Zoom).
Show pinned tabs in a separate row above: Show pinned tabs in a separate row above unpinned tabs.
Which files containing diagnostic errors: Which files containing diagnostic errors/warnings to mark in the tabs.
Controls the appearance behavior of the: Controls the appearance behavior of the tab's close button.
Whether to open tabs in preview mode whe: Whether to open tabs in preview mode when opened from the project panel with a single click.
Whether to open tabs in preview mode when selected from the file finder.
Whether to open tabs in preview mode when opened from a multibuffer.
Whether to open tabs in preview mode when code navigation is used to open a multibuffer.
Whether to open tabs in preview mode when code navigation is used to open a single file.
Whether to keep tabs in preview mode whe: Whether to keep tabs in preview mode when code navigation is used to navigate away from them. If `enable_preview_file_from_code_navigation` or `enable_preview_multibuffer_from_code_navigation` is also true, the new tab may replace the existing one.
Where to dock the project panel.
Default width of the project panel in pi: Default width of the project panel in pixels.
Whether to hide the gitignore entries in: Whether to hide the gitignore entries in the project panel.
Spacing between worktree entries in the: Spacing between worktree entries in the project panel.
Show file icons in the project panel.
Whether to show folder icons or chevrons: Whether to show folder icons or chevrons for directories in the project panel.
Show the Git status in the project panel: Show the Git status in the project panel.
Amount of indentation for nested items.
Whether to reveal entries in the project: Whether to reveal entries in the project panel automatically when a corresponding project entry becomes active.
Whether the project panel should open on: Whether the project panel should open on startup.
Whether to fold directories automaticall: Whether to fold directories automatically and show compact folders when a directory has only one subdirectory inside.
Whether to show folder names with bold t: Whether to show folder names with bold text in the project panel.
Show the scrollbar in the project panel.
Whether to allow horizontal scrolling in: Whether to allow horizontal scrolling in the project panel. When disabled, the view is always locked to the leftmost position and long file names are clipped.
Which files containing diagnostic errors: Which files containing diagnostic errors/warnings to mark in the project panel.
Show error and warning count badges next: Show error and warning count badges next to file names in the project panel.
Show a git status indicator next to file: Show a git status indicator next to file names in the project panel.
Whether to stick parent directories at t: Whether to stick parent directories at top of the project panel.
Show indent guides in the project panel.
Whether to enable drag-and-drop operatio: Whether to enable drag-and-drop operations in the project panel.
Whether to hide the root entry when only: Whether to hide the root entry when only one folder is open in the window.
Whether to hide the hidden entries in th: Whether to hide the hidden entries in the project panel.
Globs to match files that will be consid: Globs to match files that will be considered "private".
Whether to automatically open newly crea: Whether to automatically open newly created files in the editor.
Whether to automatically open files afte: Whether to automatically open files after pasting or duplicating them.
Whether to automatically open files drop: Whether to automatically open files dropped from external sources.
Sort order for entries in the project pa: Sort order for entries in the project panel.
Where to dock the terminal panel.
Show a badge on the terminal panel icon: Show a badge on the terminal panel icon with the count of open terminals.
Show the outline panel button in the sta: Show the outline panel button in the status bar.
Where to dock the outline panel.
Default width of the outline panel in pi: Default width of the outline panel in pixels.
Show file icons in the outline panel.
Whether to show folder icons or chevrons: Whether to show folder icons or chevrons for directories in the outline panel.
Show the Git status in the outline panel: Show the Git status in the outline panel.
Whether to reveal when a corresponding o: Whether to reveal when a corresponding outline entry becomes active.
Whether to fold directories automaticall: Whether to fold directories automatically when a directory contains only one subdirectory.
When to show indent guides in the outlin: When to show indent guides in the outline panel.
Show the Git panel button in the status: Show the Git panel button in the status bar.
Where to dock the Git panel.
Default width of the Git panel in pixels: Default width of the Git panel in pixels.
Default branch name will be when init.de: Default branch name will be when init.defaultbranch is not set in Git.
Enable to sort entries in the panel by p: Enable to sort entries in the panel by path, disable to sort by status.
Whether to collapse untracked files in t: Whether to collapse untracked files in the diff panel.
Enable to show entries in tree view list: Enable to show entries in tree view list, disable to show in flat view list.
Show file icons next to the Git status i: Show file icons next to the Git status icon.
Whether to show folder icons or chevrons: Whether to show folder icons or chevrons for directories in the git panel.
Whether to show the addition/deletion ch: Whether to show the addition/deletion change count next to each file in the Git panel.
Whether to show a badge on the git panel: Whether to show a badge on the git panel icon with the count of uncommitted changes.
How and when the scrollbar should be dis: How and when the scrollbar should be displayed.
Whether to show the agent panel button i: Whether to show the agent panel button in the status bar.
Where to dock the agent panel.
Default width when the agent panel is do: Default width when the agent panel is docked to the left or right.
Default height when the agent panel is d: Default height when the agent panel is docked to the bottom.
What shell to use when opening a termina: What shell to use when opening a terminal.
The arguments to pass to the shell progr: The arguments to pass to the shell program.
An optional string to override the title: An optional string to override the title of the terminal tab.
What working directory to use when launc: What working directory to use when launching the terminal.
The directory path to use (will be shell: The directory path to use (will be shell expanded).
Key-value pairs to add to the terminal's: Key-value pairs to add to the terminal's environment.
Activates the Python virtual environment: Activates the Python virtual environment, if one is found, in the terminal's working directory.
Font size for terminal text. If not set,: Font size for terminal text. If not set, defaults to buffer font size.
Font family for terminal text. If not se: Font family for terminal text. If not set, defaults to buffer font family.
Font fallbacks for terminal text. If not: Font fallbacks for terminal text. If not set, defaults to buffer font fallbacks.
Font weight for terminal text in CSS wei: Font weight for terminal text in CSS weight units (100-900).
Default cursor shape for the terminal (b: Default cursor shape for the terminal (bar, block, underline, or hollow).
Sets the cursor blinking behavior in the: Sets the cursor blinking behavior in the terminal.
Whether alternate scroll mode is active: Whether alternate scroll mode is active by default (converts mouse scroll to arrow keys in apps like Vim).
The minimum APCA perceptual contrast bet: The minimum APCA perceptual contrast between foreground and background colors (0-106).
Whether the option key behaves as the me: Whether the option key behaves as the meta key.
Whether selecting text in the terminal a: Whether selecting text in the terminal automatically copies to the system clipboard.
Whether to keep the text selection after: Whether to keep the text selection after copying it to the clipboard.
Default width when the terminal is docke: Default width when the terminal is docked to the left or right (in pixels).
Default height when the terminal is dock: Default height when the terminal is docked to the bottom (in pixels).
Maximum number of lines to keep in scrol: Maximum number of lines to keep in scrollback history (max: 100,000; 0 disables scrolling).
The multiplier for scrolling in the term: The multiplier for scrolling in the terminal with the mouse wheel
Display the terminal title in breadcrumb: Display the terminal title in breadcrumbs inside the terminal pane.
When to show the scrollbar in the termin: When to show the scrollbar in the terminal.
Disable all Git integration features in: Disable all Git integration features in Dokkaebi.
Show Git status information in the edito: Show Git status information in the editor.
Show Git diff information in the editor.
Control whether Git status is shown in t: Control whether Git status is shown in the editor's gutter.
Debounce threshold in milliseconds after: Debounce threshold in milliseconds after which changes are reflected in the Git gutter.
Whether or not to show Git blame data in: Whether or not to show Git blame data inline in the currently focused line.
The delay after which the inline blame i: The delay after which the inline blame information is shown.
Padding between the end of the source li: Padding between the end of the source line and the start of the inline blame in columns.
The minimum column number at which to sh: The minimum column number at which to show the inline blame information.
Show commit summary as part of the inlin: Show commit summary as part of the inline blame.
Show the avatar of the author of the com: Show the avatar of the author of the commit.
Show author name as part of the commit i: Show author name as part of the commit information in branch picker.
How Git hunks are displayed visually in: How Git hunks are displayed visually in the editor.
Should the name or path be displayed fir: Should the name or path be displayed first in the git view.
Whether the microphone should be muted w: Whether the microphone should be muted when joining a channel or a call.
Whether your current project should be s: Whether your current project should be shared when joining an empty channel.
Whether to disable all AI features in Ze: Whether to disable all AI features in Dokkaebi.
Whether to start a new thread in the cur: Whether to start a new thread in the current local project or in a new Git worktree.
When enabled, agent edits will also be d: When enabled, agent edits will also be displayed in single-file buffers for review.
Show voting thumbs up/down icon buttons: Show voting thumbs up/down icon buttons for feedback on agent edits.
Where to show notifications when the age: Where to show notifications when the agent has completed its response or needs confirmation before running a tool action.
Whether to play a sound when the agent h: Whether to play a sound when the agent has either completed its response, or needs user input.
Whether to have edit cards in the agent: Whether to have edit cards in the agent panel expanded, showing a Preview of the diff.
Whether to have terminal cards in the ag: Whether to have terminal cards in the agent panel expanded, showing the whole command output.
Whether clicking the stop button on a ru: Whether clicking the stop button on a running terminal tool should also cancel the agent's generation. Note that this only applies to the stop button, not to ctrl+c inside the terminal.
Whether to always use cmd-enter (or ctrl: Whether to always use cmd-enter (or ctrl-enter on Linux or Windows) to send messages.
Minimum number of lines to display in th: Minimum number of lines to display in the agent message editor.
Whether to show turn statistics like ela: Whether to show turn statistics like elapsed time during generation and final turn duration.
Default timeout in seconds for context s: Default timeout in seconds for context server tool calls. Can be overridden per-server in context_servers configuration.
When to show edit predictions previews i: When to show edit predictions previews in buffer. The eager mode displays them inline, while the subtle mode displays them only when holding a modifier key.
Whether edit predictions are enabled whe: Whether edit predictions are enabled when editing text threads in the agent panel.
How many columns a tab should occupy.
Whether to indent lines using tab charac: Whether to indent lines using tab characters, as opposed to multiple spaces.
Controls automatic indentation behavior: Controls automatic indentation behavior when typing.
Whether indentation of pasted content sh: Whether indentation of pasted content should be adjusted based on the context.
How to soft-wrap long lines of text.
Show wrap guides in the editor.
The column at which to soft-wrap lines,: The column at which to soft-wrap lines, for buffers where soft-wrap is enabled.
Character counts at which to show wrap g: Character counts at which to show wrap guides in the editor.
Controls where the `editor::rewrap` acti: Controls where the `editor::rewrap` action is allowed for this language.
Display indent guides in the editor.
The width of the indent guides in pixels: The width of the indent guides in pixels, between 1 and 10.
The width of the active indent guide in: The width of the active indent guide in pixels, between 1 and 10.
Determines how indent guides are colored: Determines how indent guides are colored.
Determines how indent guide backgrounds: Determines how indent guide backgrounds are colored.
Whether or not to perform a buffer forma: Whether or not to perform a buffer format before saving.
Whether or not to remove any trailing wh: Whether or not to remove any trailing whitespace from lines of a buffer before saving it.
Whether or not to ensure there's a singl: Whether or not to ensure there's a single newline at the end of a buffer when saving it.
How to perform a buffer format.
Whether to use additional LSP queries to: Whether to use additional LSP queries to format (and amend) the code after every "format_on_save" trigger.
Additional code actions to run when form: Additional code actions to run when formatting.
Whether to automatically type closing ch: Whether to automatically type closing characters for you. For example, when you type '(', Dokkaebi will automatically add a closing ')' at the correct position.
Whether to automatically surround text w: Whether to automatically surround text with characters for you. For example, when you select text and type '(', Dokkaebi will automatically surround text with ().
Controls whether the closing characters: Controls whether the closing characters are always skipped over and auto-removed no matter how they were inserted.
Whether to automatically close JSX tags.
Whether to show tabs and spaces in the e: Whether to show tabs and spaces in the editor.
Visible character used to render space c: Visible character used to render space characters when show_whitespaces is enabled (default: "·").
Visible character used to render tab cha: Visible character used to render tab characters when show_whitespaces is enabled (default: "→").
Whether to pop the completions menu whil: Whether to pop the completions menu while typing in an editor without explicitly requesting it.
Whether to display inline and alongside: Whether to display inline and alongside documentation for items in the completions menu.
How many characters has to be in the com: How many characters has to be in the completions query to automatically show the words-based completions.
When to show the scrollbar in the comple: When to show the scrollbar in the completion menu.
Whether to align detail text in code com: Whether to align detail text in code completions context menus left or right.
Global switch to toggle hints on and off: Global switch to toggle hints on and off.
Whether type hints should be shown.
Whether parameter hints should be shown.
Whether other hints should be shown.
Show a background for inlay hints.
Whether or not to debounce inlay hints u: Whether or not to debounce inlay hints updates after buffer edits (set to 0 to disable debouncing).
Whether or not to debounce inlay hints updates after buffer scrolls (set to 0 to disable debouncing).
Toggles inlay hints (hides or shows) whe: Toggles inlay hints (hides or shows) when the user presses the modifiers specified.
Whether tasks are enabled for this langu: Whether tasks are enabled for this language.
Extra task variables to set for a partic: Extra task variables to set for a particular language.
Use LSP tasks over Zed language extensio: Use LSP tasks over Dokkaebi language extension tasks.
Whether to enable word diff highlighting: Whether to enable word diff highlighting in the editor. When enabled, changed words within modified lines are highlighted to show exactly what changed.
Whether to start a new line with a comme: Whether to start a new line with a comment when a previous line is a comment as well.
Whether to colorize brackets in the edit: Whether to colorize brackets in the editor.
Number of lines to search for modelines: Number of lines to search for modelines (set to 0 to disable).
The unit for image file sizes.
Whether to automatically replace emoji s: Whether to automatically replace emoji shortcodes with emoji characters.
Relative size of the drop target in the: Relative size of the drop target in the editor that will open dropped file as a split pane.
How to render LSP color previews in the: How to render LSP color previews in the editor.
Whether to use language servers to provi: Whether to use language servers to provide code intelligence.
The list of language servers to use (or: The list of language servers to use (or disable) for this language.
Whether to perform linked edits of assoc: Whether to perform linked edits of associated ranges, if the LS supports it. For example, when editing opening <html> tag, the contents of the closing </html> tag will be edited as well.
Whether to follow-up empty Go to definit: Whether to follow-up empty Go to definition responses from the language server.
When enabled, use folding ranges from th: When enabled, use folding ranges from the language server instead of indent-based folding.
When enabled, use the language server's: When enabled, use the language server's document symbols for outlines and breadcrumbs instead of tree-sitter.
Whether to fetch LSP completions or not.
When fetching LSP completions, determine: When fetching LSP completions, determines how long to wait for a response of a particular server (set to 0 to wait indefinitely).
Controls how LSP completions are inserte: Controls how LSP completions are inserted.
Enables or disables formatting with Pret: Enables or disables formatting with Prettier for a given language.
Forces Prettier integration to use a spe: Forces Prettier integration to use a specific parser name when formatting files with the language.
Forces Prettier integration to use speci: Forces Prettier integration to use specific plugins when formatting files with the language.
Default Prettier options, in the format: Default Prettier options, in the format as in package.json section for Prettier.
Controls whether edit predictions are sh: Controls whether edit predictions are shown immediately or manually.
Controls whether edit predictions are shown in the given language scopes.
Display a background image in the worksp: Display a background image in the workspace.
How the background image is sized to fit: How the background image is sized to fit the workspace.
Opacity of editor and terminal backgroun: Opacity of editor and terminal backgrounds when wallpaper is enabled. Lower values make the wallpaper more visible.
settings_page.language.semantic_tokens.d:
Off: Do not request semantic tokens from language servers.
Combined: Use LSP semantic tokens together with tree-sitter highlighting.
Full: Use LSP semantic tokens exclusively, replacing tree-sitter highlighting.
settings_page.desc.base_keymap.base_keym: The name of a base set of key bindings to use.
settings_page.desc.modal_editing.vim_mod: Enable Vim mode and key bindings.
settings_page.desc.modal_editing.helix_m: Enable Helix mode and key bindings.
settings_page.desc.general_settings.proj: The displayed name of this project. If left empty, the root directory name will be displayed.
settings_page.desc.general_settings.when: What to do when using the 'close active item' action with no tabs.
settings_page.desc.general_settings.on_l: What to do when the last window is closed.
settings_page.desc.general_settings.use_: Use native OS dialogs for 'Open' and 'Save As'.
Use native OS dialogs for confirmations.
settings_page.desc.general_settings.syst: Show CPU, memory, and GPU usage in the status bar.
settings_page.desc.general_settings.auto: Launch Dokkaebi automatically when Windows starts.
settings_page.desc.general_settings.loca: Select the language used in the UI.
settings_page.desc.workspace_restoration: Whether or not to restore unsaved buffers on restart.
What to restore from the previous session when opening the app.
settings_page.desc.auto_update.auto_upda: Checks for the latest updates when the app starts.
settings_page.desc.auto_update.show_rele: Show release notes once on the first launch after an update.
settings_page.desc.file_types.file_type_: A mapping from languages to files and file extensions that should be treated as that language.
settings_page.desc.diagnostics.max_sever: Which level to use to filter out diagnostics displayed in the editor.
settings_page.desc.diagnostics.include_w: Whether to show warnings or not by default.
settings_page.desc.inline_diagnostics.en: Whether to show diagnostics inline or not.
settings_page.desc.inline_diagnostics.up: The delay in milliseconds to show inline diagnostics after the last diagnostic update.
settings_page.desc.inline_diagnostics.pa: The amount of padding between the end of the source line and the start of the inline diagnostic.
settings_page.desc.inline_diagnostics.mi: The minimum column at which to display inline diagnostics.
Whether to pull for language server-powered diagnostics or not.
Minimum time to wait before pulling diagnostics from the language server(s).
settings_page.desc.lsp_highlights.deboun: The debounce delay before querying highlights from the language.
settings_page.desc.git_integration.enabl: Show Git status information in the editor.
Show Git diff information in the editor.
settings_page.desc.git_gutter.visibility: Control whether Git status is shown in the editor's gutter.
settings_page.desc.git_gutter.debounce: Debounce threshold in milliseconds after which changes are reflected in the Git gutter.
settings_page.desc.inline_git_blame.enab: Whether or not to show Git blame data inline in the currently focused line.
settings_page.desc.inline_git_blame.dela: The delay after which the inline blame information is shown.
settings_page.desc.inline_git_blame.padd: Padding between the end of the source line and the start of the inline blame in columns.
settings_page.desc.inline_git_blame.mini: The minimum column number at which to show the inline blame information.
settings_page.desc.inline_git_blame.show: Show commit summary as part of the inline blame.
settings_page.desc.git_blame_view.show_a: Show the avatar of the author of the commit.
settings_page.desc.branch_picker.show_au: Show author name as part of the commit information in branch picker.
settings_page.desc.git_hunks.hunk_style: How Git hunks are displayed visually in the editor.
settings_page.desc.git_hunks.path_style: Should the name or path be displayed first in the git view.
settings_page.desc.general.disable_ai: Whether to disable all AI features.
settings_page.desc.agent_configuration.n: Whether to start a new thread in the current local project or in a new Git worktree.
settings_page.desc.agent_configuration.s: When enabled, agent edits will also be displayed in single-file buffers for review.
settings_page.desc.agent_configuration.e: Show voting thumbs up/down icon buttons for feedback on agent edits.
settings_page.desc.agent_configuration.n: Where to show notifications when the agent has completed its response or needs confirmation before running a tool action.
settings_page.desc.agent_configuration.p: Whether to play a sound when the agent has either completed its response, or needs user input.
settings_page.desc.agent_configuration.e: Whether to have edit cards in the agent panel expanded, showing a Preview of the diff.
Whether to have terminal cards in the agent panel expanded, showing the whole command output.
settings_page.desc.agent_configuration.c: Whether clicking the stop button on a running terminal tool should also cancel the agent's generation. Note that this only applies to the stop button, not to ctrl+c inside the terminal.
settings_page.desc.agent_configuration.u: Whether to always use cmd-enter (or ctrl-enter on Linux or Windows) to send messages.
settings_page.desc.agent_configuration.m: Minimum number of lines to display in the agent message editor.
settings_page.desc.agent_configuration.s: Whether to show turn statistics like elapsed time during generation and final turn duration.
settings_page.desc.context_servers.conte: Default timeout in seconds for context server tool calls. Can be overridden per-server in context_servers configuration.
settings_page.desc.edit_prediction_displ: When to show edit predictions previews in buffer. The eager mode displays them inline, while the subtle mode displays them only when holding a modifier key.
Whether edit predictions are enabled when editing text threads in the agent panel.
settings_page.desc.search.whole_word: Search for whole words by default.
settings_page.desc.search.use_smartcase_: Whether to automatically enable case-sensitive search based on the search query.
settings_page.desc.search.include_ignore: Include ignored files in search results by default.
settings_page.desc.search.search_wrap: Whether the editor search results will loop.
settings_page.desc.search.center_on_matc: Whether to center the current match in the editor
settings_page.desc.search.seed_search_qu: When to populate a new search's query based on the text under the cursor.
settings_page.desc.file_finder.file_icon: Show file icons in the file finder.
settings_page.desc.file_finder.modal_max: Determines how much space the file finder can take up in relation to the available window width.
settings_page.desc.file_finder.skip_focu: Whether the file finder should skip focus for the active file in search results.
settings_page.desc.file_scan.file_scan_e: Files or globs of files that will be excluded entirely. They will be skipped during file scans, file searches, and not be displayed in the project file tree. Takes precedence over "File Scan Inclusions"
settings_page.desc.file_scan.file_scan_i: Files or globs of files that will be included even when ignored by git. This is useful for files that are not tracked by git, but are still important to your project. Note that globs that are overly broad can slow down file scanning. "File Scan Exclusions" takes precedence over these inclusions
settings_page.desc.file_scan.restore_fil: Restore previous file state when reopening.
settings_page.desc.file_scan.close_on_fi: Automatically close files that have been deleted.
settings_page.desc.environment.shell: What shell to use when opening a terminal.
settings_page.desc.environment.arguments: The arguments to pass to the shell program.
settings_page.desc.environment.title_ove: An optional string to override the title of the terminal tab.
settings_page.desc.environment.working_d: What working directory to use when launching the terminal.
settings_page.desc.environment.directory: The directory path to use (will be shell expanded).
settings_page.desc.environment.environme: Key-value pairs to add to the terminal's environment.
settings_page.desc.environment.detect_vi: Activates the Python virtual environment, if one is found, in the terminal's working directory.
settings_page.desc.font.font_size: Font size for terminal text. If not set, defaults to buffer font size.
settings_page.desc.font.font_family: Font family for terminal text. If not set, defaults to buffer font family.
settings_page.desc.font.font_fallbacks: Font fallbacks for terminal text. If not set, defaults to buffer font fallbacks.
settings_page.desc.font.font_weight: Font weight for terminal text in CSS weight units (100-900).
settings_page.desc.display_settings.curs: Default cursor shape for the terminal (bar, block, underline, or hollow).
Sets the cursor blinking behavior in the terminal.
settings_page.desc.display_settings.alte: Whether alternate scroll mode is active by default (converts mouse scroll to arrow keys in apps like Vim).
settings_page.desc.display_settings.mini: The minimum APCA perceptual contrast between foreground and background colors (0-106).
settings_page.desc.behavior_settings.cop: Whether selecting text in the terminal automatically copies to the system clipboard.
settings_page.desc.behavior_settings.kee: Whether to keep the text selection after copying it to the clipboard.
settings_page.desc.layout_settings.defau: Default width when the terminal is docked to the left or right (in pixels).
Default height when the terminal is docked to the bottom (in pixels).
settings_page.desc.advanced_settings.max: Maximum number of lines to keep in scrollback history (max: 100,000; 0 disables scrolling).
settings_page.desc.advanced_settings.scr: The multiplier for scrolling in the terminal with the mouse wheel
settings_page.desc.terminal_toolbar.brea: Display the terminal title in breadcrumbs inside the terminal pane.
settings_page.desc.terminal_scrollbar.sh: When to show the scrollbar in the terminal.
settings_page.desc.status_bar.project_pa: Show the project panel button in the status bar.
settings_page.desc.status_bar.active_lan: Show the active language button in the status bar.
settings_page.desc.status_bar.active_enc: Control when to show the active encoding in the status bar.
settings_page.desc.status_bar.cursor_pos: Show the cursor position button in the status bar.
settings_page.desc.status_bar.terminal_b: Show the terminal button in the status bar.
settings_page.desc.status_bar.diagnostic: Show the project diagnostics button in the status bar.
settings_page.desc.status_bar.project_se: Show the project search button in the status bar.
settings_page.desc.status_bar.active_fil: Show the name of the active file in the status bar.
settings_page.desc.tab_bar.show_tab_bar: Show the tab bar in the editor.
settings_page.desc.tab_bar.show_git_stat: Show the Git file status on a tab item.
settings_page.desc.tab_bar.show_file_ico: Show the file icon for a tab.
settings_page.desc.tab_bar.tab_close_pos: Position of the close button in a tab.
settings_page.desc.tab_bar.maximum_tabs: Maximum open tabs in a pane. Will not close an unsaved tab.
settings_page.desc.tab_bar.show_navigati: Show the navigation history buttons in the tab bar.
settings_page.desc.tab_bar.show_tab_bar_: Show the tab bar buttons (New, Split Pane, Zoom).
settings_page.desc.tab_bar.pinned_tabs_l: Show pinned tabs in a separate row above unpinned tabs.
settings_page.desc.tab_settings.activate: What to do after closing the current tab.
settings_page.desc.tab_settings.tab_show: Which files containing diagnostic errors/warnings to mark in the tabs.
settings_page.desc.tab_settings.show_clo: Controls the appearance behavior of the tab's close button.
settings_page.desc.preview_tabs.preview_: Show opened editors as preview tabs.
settings_page.desc.preview_tabs.enable_p: Whether to open tabs in preview mode when opened from the project panel with a single click.
Whether to open tabs in preview mode when selected from the file finder.
Whether to open tabs in preview mode when opened from a multibuffer.
Whether to open tabs in preview mode when code navigation is used to open a multibuffer.
Whether to open tabs in preview mode when code navigation is used to open a single file.
settings_page.desc.preview_tabs.enable_k: Whether to keep tabs in preview mode when code navigation is used to navigate away from them. If `enable_preview_file_from_code_navigation` or `enable_preview_multibuffer_from_code_navigation` is also true, the new tab may replace the existing one.
settings_page.desc.layout.bottom_dock_la: Layout mode for the bottom dock.
settings_page.desc.pane_modifiers.border: Size of the border surrounding the active pane.
settings_page.desc.theme.theme_mode: Choose a static, fixed theme or dynamically select themes based on appearance and light/dark modes.
settings_page.desc.theme.theme_name: The name of your selected theme.
settings_page.desc.theme.mode: Choose whether to use the selected light or dark theme or to follow your OS appearance configuration.
settings_page.desc.theme.light_theme: The theme to use when mode is set to light, or when mode is set to system and it is in light mode.
settings_page.desc.theme.dark_theme: The theme to use when mode is set to dark, or when mode is set to system and it is in dark mode.
settings_page.desc.theme.icon_theme: The custom set of icons that will be associated with files and directories.
settings_page.desc.theme.icon_theme_name: The name of your selected icon theme.
settings_page.desc.icon_theme.mode: Choose whether to use the selected light or dark icon theme or to follow your OS appearance configuration.
settings_page.desc.theme.light_icon_them: The icon theme to use when mode is set to light, or when mode is set to system and it is in light mode.
settings_page.desc.theme.dark_icon_theme: The icon theme to use when mode is set to dark, or when mode is set to system and it is in dark mode.
settings_page.desc.buffer_font.custom_li: Custom line height value (must be at least 1.0).
settings_page.desc.buffer_font.font_feat: The OpenType features to enable for rendering in text buffers.
settings_page.desc.buffer_font.font_fall: The font fallbacks to use for rendering in text buffers.
settings_page.desc.ui_font.font_features: The OpenType features to enable for rendering in UI elements.
settings_page.desc.ui_font.font_fallback: The font fallbacks to use for rendering in the UI.
settings_page.desc.agent_panel_font.ui_f: Font size for agent response text in the agent panel. Falls back to the regular UI font size.
settings_page.desc.agent_panel_font.buff: Font size for user messages text in the agent panel.
settings_page.desc.text_rendering.text_r: The text rendering mode to use.
settings_page.desc.cursor.multi_cursor_m: Modifier key for adding multiple cursors.
settings_page.desc.cursor.cursor_blink: Whether the cursor blinks in the editor.
settings_page.desc.cursor.hide_mouse: When to hide the mouse cursor.
settings_page.desc.highlighting.unnecess: How much to fade out unused code (0.0 - 0.9).
settings_page.desc.highlighting.current_: How to highlight the current line.
settings_page.desc.highlighting.selectio: Highlight all occurrences of selected text.
settings_page.desc.highlighting.rounded_: Whether the text selection should have rounded corners.
settings_page.desc.highlighting.minimum_: The minimum APCA perceptual contrast to maintain when rendering text over highlight backgrounds.
settings_page.desc.guides.wrap_guides: Character counts at which to show wrap guides.
settings_page.desc.project_panel.project: Where to dock the project panel.
Default width of the project panel in pixels.
settings_page.desc.project_panel.hide_gi: Whether to hide the gitignore entries in the project panel.
settings_page.desc.project_panel.entry_s: Spacing between worktree entries in the project panel.
settings_page.desc.project_panel.file_ic: Show file icons in the project panel.
settings_page.desc.project_panel.folder_: Whether to show folder icons or chevrons for directories in the project panel.
settings_page.desc.project_panel.git_sta: Show the Git status in the project panel.
settings_page.desc.project_panel.indent_: Amount of indentation for nested items.
settings_page.desc.project_panel.auto_re: Whether to reveal entries in the project panel automatically when a corresponding project entry becomes active.
settings_page.desc.project_panel.starts_: Whether the project panel should open on startup.
settings_page.desc.project_panel.auto_fo: Whether to fold directories automatically and show compact folders when a directory has only one subdirectory inside.
settings_page.desc.project_panel.bold_fo: Whether to show folder names with bold text in the project panel.
settings_page.desc.project_panel.show_sc: Show the scrollbar in the project panel.
settings_page.desc.project_panel.horizon: Whether to allow horizontal scrolling in the project panel. When disabled, the view is always locked to the leftmost position and long file names are clipped.
settings_page.desc.project_panel.show_di: Which files containing diagnostic errors/warnings to mark in the project panel.
settings_page.desc.project_panel.diagnos: Show error and warning count badges next to file names in the project panel.
settings_page.desc.project_panel.git_sta: Show a git status indicator next to file names in the project panel.
settings_page.desc.project_panel.sticky_: Whether to stick parent directories at top of the project panel.
settings_page.desc.project_panel.show_in: Show indent guides in the project panel.
settings_page.desc.project_panel.drag_an: Whether to enable drag-and-drop operations in the project panel.
settings_page.desc.project_panel.hide_ro: Whether to hide the root entry when only one folder is open in the window.
settings_page.desc.project_panel.hide_hi: Whether to hide the hidden entries in the project panel.
settings_page.desc.project_panel.hidden_: Globs to match files that will be considered \"hidden\" and can be hidden from the project panel.
settings_page.desc.auto_open_files.on_cr: Whether to automatically open newly created files in the editor.
settings_page.desc.auto_open_files.on_pa: Whether to automatically open files after pasting or duplicating them.
settings_page.desc.auto_open_files.on_dr: Whether to automatically open files dropped from external sources.
settings_page.desc.auto_open_files.sort_: Sort order for entries in the project panel.
settings_page.desc.terminal_panel.termin: Where to dock the terminal panel.
settings_page.desc.terminal_panel.show_c: Show a badge on the terminal panel icon with the count of open terminals.
settings_page.desc.outline_panel.outline: Show the outline panel button in the status bar.
Where to dock the outline panel.
Default width of the outline panel in pixels.
settings_page.desc.outline_panel.file_ic: Show file icons in the outline panel.
settings_page.desc.outline_panel.folder_: Whether to show folder icons or chevrons for directories in the outline panel.
settings_page.desc.outline_panel.git_sta: Show the Git status in the outline panel.
settings_page.desc.outline_panel.indent_: Amount of indentation for nested items.
settings_page.desc.outline_panel.auto_re: Whether to reveal when a corresponding outline entry becomes active.
settings_page.desc.outline_panel.auto_fo: Whether to fold directories automatically when a directory contains only one subdirectory.
settings_page.desc.outline_panel.show_in: When to show indent guides in the outline panel.
settings_page.desc.git_panel.git_panel_b: Show the Git panel button in the status bar.
settings_page.desc.git_panel.git_panel_d: Where to dock the Git panel.
Default width of the Git panel in pixels.
settings_page.desc.git_panel.fallback_br: Default branch name will be when init.defaultbranch is not set in Git.
settings_page.desc.git_panel.sort_by_pat: Enable to sort entries in the panel by path, disable to sort by status.
settings_page.desc.git_panel.collapse_un: Whether to collapse untracked files in the diff panel.
settings_page.desc.git_panel.tree_view: Enable to show entries in tree view list, disable to show in flat view list.
settings_page.desc.git_panel.file_icons: Show file icons next to the Git status icon.
settings_page.desc.git_panel.folder_icon: Whether to show folder icons or chevrons for directories in the git panel.
settings_page.desc.git_panel.diff_stats: Whether to show the addition/deletion change count next to each file in the Git panel.
settings_page.desc.git_panel.show_count_: Whether to show a badge on the git panel icon with the count of uncommitted changes.
settings_page.desc.git_panel.scroll_bar: How and when the scrollbar should be displayed.
settings_page.desc.agent_panel.agent_pan: Whether to show the agent panel button in the status bar.
Where to dock the agent panel.
Default width when the agent panel is docked to the left or right.
Default height when the agent panel is docked to the bottom.
settings_page.desc.auto_save.auto_save_m: When to auto save buffer changes.
settings_page.desc.which_key.show_which_: Display the which-key menu with matching bindings while a multi-stroke binding is pending.
settings_page.desc.which_key.menu_delay: Delay in milliseconds before the which-key menu appears.
settings_page.desc.multibuffer.double_cl: What to do when multibuffer is double-clicked in some of its excerpts.
settings_page.desc.multibuffer.expand_ex: How many lines to expand the multibuffer excerpts by default.
settings_page.desc.multibuffer.excerpt_c: How many lines of context to provide in multibuffer excerpts by default.
settings_page.desc.multibuffer.expand_ou: Default depth to expand outline items in the current file.
settings_page.desc.multibuffer.diff_view: How to display diffs in the editor.
settings_page.desc.scrolling.scroll_beyo: Whether the editor will scroll beyond the last line.
settings_page.desc.scrolling.vertical_sc: The number of lines to keep above/below the cursor when auto-scrolling.
settings_page.desc.scrolling.horizontal_: The number of characters to keep on either side when scrolling with the mouse.
settings_page.desc.scrolling.scroll_sens: Scroll sensitivity multiplier for both horizontal and vertical scrolling.
settings_page.desc.scrolling.fast_scroll: Fast scroll sensitivity multiplier for both horizontal and vertical scrolling.
settings_page.desc.scrolling.autoscroll_: Whether to scroll when clicking near the edge of the visible text area.
settings_page.desc.scrolling.sticky_scro: Whether to stick scopes to the top of the editor
settings_page.desc.signature_help.auto_s: Automatically show a signature help pop-up.
settings_page.desc.signature_help.show_s: Show the signature help pop-up after completions or bracket pairs are inserted.
settings_page.desc.signature_help.snippe: Determines how snippets are sorted relative to other completion items.
settings_page.desc.hover_popover.enabled: Show the informational hover box when moving the mouse over symbols in the editor.
settings_page.desc.hover_popover.delay: Time to wait in milliseconds before showing the informational hover box.
settings_page.desc.drag_and_drop_selecti: Delay in milliseconds before drag and drop selection starts.
settings_page.desc.gutter.show_line_numb: Show line numbers in the gutter.
settings_page.desc.gutter.relative_line_: Controls line number display in the editor's gutter. \"disabled\" shows absolute line numbers, \"enabled\" shows relative line numbers for each absolute line, and \"wrapped\" shows relative line numbers for every line, absolute or wrapped.
settings_page.desc.gutter.show_runnables: Show runnable buttons in the gutter.
settings_page.desc.gutter.show_folds: Show code folding controls in the gutter.
settings_page.desc.gutter.min_line_numbe: Minimum number of characters to reserve space for in the gutter.
settings_page.desc.gutter.inline_code_ac: Show code action button at start of buffer line.
settings_page.desc.scrollbar.show: When to show the scrollbar in the editor.
settings_page.desc.scrollbar.cursors: Show cursor positions in the scrollbar.
settings_page.desc.scrollbar.git_diff: Show Git diff indicators in the scrollbar.
settings_page.desc.scrollbar.search_resu: Show buffer search result indicators in the scrollbar.
settings_page.desc.scrollbar.selected_te: Show selected text occurrences in the scrollbar.
settings_page.desc.scrollbar.selected_sy: Show selected symbol occurrences in the scrollbar.
settings_page.desc.scrollbar.diagnostics: Which diagnostic indicators to show in the scrollbar.
settings_page.desc.scrollbar.horizontal_: When false, forcefully disables the horizontal scrollbar.
settings_page.desc.scrollbar.vertical_sc: When false, forcefully disables the vertical scrollbar.
settings_page.desc.minimap.show: When to show the minimap in the editor.
settings_page.desc.minimap.display_in: Where to show the minimap in the editor.
settings_page.desc.minimap.thumb: When to show the minimap thumb.
settings_page.desc.minimap.thumb_border: Border style for the minimap's scrollbar thumb.
settings_page.desc.minimap.current_line_: How to highlight the current line in the minimap.
settings_page.desc.minimap.max_width_col: Maximum number of columns to display in the minimap.
settings_page.desc.toolbar.quick_actions: Show quick action buttons (e.g., search, selection, editor controls, etc.).
settings_page.desc.toolbar.selections_me: Show the selections menu in the editor toolbar.
settings_page.desc.toolbar.agent_review: Show agent review buttons in the editor toolbar.
settings_page.desc.toolbar.code_actions: Show code action buttons in the editor toolbar.
settings_page.desc.vim_settings.default_: The default mode when Vim starts.
settings_page.desc.vim_settings.toggle_r: Toggle relative line numbers in Vim mode.
settings_page.desc.vim_settings.use_syst: Controls when to use system clipboard in Vim mode.
settings_page.desc.vim_settings.use_smar: Enable smartcase searching in Vim mode.
settings_page.desc.vim_settings.global_s: When enabled, the :substitute command replaces all matches in a line by default. The 'g' flag then toggles this behavior.
settings_page.desc.vim_settings.highligh: Duration in milliseconds to highlight yanked text in Vim mode.
settings_page.desc.vim_settings.cursor_s: Cursor shape for insert mode. Inherit uses the editor's cursor shape.
settings_page.desc.vim_settings.custom_d: Custom digraph mappings for Vim mode.
settings_page.desc.indentation.tab_size: How many columns a tab should occupy.
settings_page.desc.indentation.hard_tabs: Whether to indent lines using tab characters, as opposed to multiple spaces.
settings_page.desc.indentation.auto_inde: Controls automatic indentation behavior when typing.
Whether indentation of pasted content should be adjusted based on the context.
settings_page.desc.wrapping.soft_wrap: How to soft-wrap long lines of text.
settings_page.desc.wrapping.show_wrap_gu: Show wrap guides in the editor.
settings_page.desc.wrapping.preferred_li: The column at which to soft-wrap lines, for buffers where soft-wrap is enabled.
settings_page.desc.wrapping.wrap_guides: Character counts at which to show wrap guides in the editor.
settings_page.desc.wrapping.allow_rewrap: Controls where the `editor::rewrap` action is allowed for this language.
settings_page.desc.indent_guides.enabled: Display indent guides in the editor.
settings_page.desc.indent_guides.line_wi: The width of the indent guides in pixels, between 1 and 10.
settings_page.desc.indent_guides.active_: The width of the active indent guide in pixels, between 1 and 10.
settings_page.desc.indent_guides.colorin: Determines how indent guides are colored.
settings_page.desc.indent_guides.backgro: Determines how indent guide backgrounds are colored.
settings_page.desc.formatting.format_on_: Whether or not to perform a buffer format before saving.
settings_page.desc.formatting.remove_tra: Whether or not to remove any trailing whitespace from lines of a buffer before saving it.
settings_page.desc.formatting.ensure_fin: Whether or not to ensure there's a single newline at the end of a buffer when saving it.
settings_page.desc.formatting.formatter: How to perform a buffer format.
settings_page.desc.formatting.use_on_typ: Whether to use additional LSP queries to format (and amend) the code after every \"trigger\" symbol input, defined by LSP server capabilities
settings_page.desc.formatting.code_actio: Additional code actions to run when formatting.
settings_page.desc.autoclose.use_autoclo: Whether to automatically type closing characters for you. For example, when you type '(', a closing ')' is automatically added at the correct position.
settings_page.desc.autoclose.use_auto_su: Whether to automatically surround text with characters for you. For example, when you select text and type '(', the text is automatically surrounded with ().
settings_page.desc.autoclose.always_trea: Controls whether the closing characters are always skipped over and auto-removed no matter how they were inserted.
settings_page.desc.autoclose.jsx_tag_aut: Whether to automatically close JSX tags.
settings_page.desc.whitespace.show_white: Whether to show tabs and spaces in the editor.
settings_page.desc.whitespace.space_whit: Visible character used to render space characters when show_whitespaces is enabled (default: \"•\")
settings_page.desc.whitespace.tab_whites: Visible character used to render tab characters when show_whitespaces is enabled (default: \"→\")
settings_page.desc.completions.show_comp: Whether to pop the completions menu while typing in an editor without explicitly requesting it.
Whether to display inline and alongside documentation for items in the completions menu.
settings_page.desc.completions.words_min: How many characters has to be in the completions query to automatically show the words-based completions.
settings_page.desc.completions.completio: When to show the scrollbar in the completion menu.
Whether to align detail text in code completions context menus left or right.
settings_page.desc.inlay_hints.enabled: Global switch to toggle hints on and off.
settings_page.desc.inlay_hints.show_valu: Global switch to toggle inline values on and off when debugging.
settings_page.desc.inlay_hints.show_type: Whether type hints should be shown.
settings_page.desc.inlay_hints.show_para: Whether parameter hints should be shown.
settings_page.desc.inlay_hints.show_othe: Whether other hints should be shown.
settings_page.desc.inlay_hints.show_back: Show a background for inlay hints.
settings_page.desc.inlay_hints.edit_debo: Whether or not to debounce inlay hints updates after buffer edits (set to 0 to disable debouncing).
settings_page.desc.inlay_hints.scroll_de: Whether or not to debounce inlay hints updates after buffer scrolls (set to 0 to disable debouncing).
settings_page.desc.inlay_hints.toggle_on: Toggles inlay hints (hides or shows) when the user presses the modifiers specified.
settings_page.desc.tasks.enabled: Whether tasks are enabled for this language.
settings_page.desc.tasks.variables: Extra task variables to set for a particular language.
settings_page.desc.tasks.prefer_lsp: Use LSP tasks over language extension tasks.
settings_page.desc.miscellaneous.word_di: Whether to enable word diff highlighting in the editor. When enabled, changed words within modified lines are highlighted to show exactly what changed.
settings_page.desc.miscellaneous.extend_: Whether to start a new line with a comment when a previous line is a comment as well.
settings_page.desc.miscellaneous.coloriz: Whether to colorize brackets in the editor.
settings_page.desc.miscellaneous.vim_ema: Number of lines to search for modelines (set to 0 to disable).
settings_page.desc.global_only_miscellan: The unit for image file sizes.
Whether to automatically replace emoji shortcodes with emoji characters.
Relative size of the drop target in the editor that will open dropped file as a split pane.
How to render LSP color previews in the editor.
settings_page.desc.lsp.enable_language_s: Whether to use language servers to provide code intelligence.
settings_page.desc.lsp.language_servers: The list of language servers to use (or disable) for this language.
settings_page.desc.lsp.linked_edits: Whether to perform linked edits of associated ranges, if the LS supports it. For example, when editing opening <html> tag, the contents of the closing </html> tag will be edited as well.
settings_page.desc.lsp.go_to_definition_: Whether to follow-up empty Go to definition responses from the language server.
settings_page.desc.lsp.lsp_folding_range: When enabled, use folding ranges from the language server instead of indent-based folding.
settings_page.desc.lsp.lsp_document_symb: When enabled, use the language server's document symbols for outlines and breadcrumbs instead of tree-sitter.
settings_page.desc.lsp_completions.enabl: Whether to fetch LSP completions or not.
settings_page.desc.lsp_completions.fetch: When fetching LSP completions, determines how long to wait for a response of a particular server (set to 0 to wait indefinitely).
settings_page.desc.lsp_completions.inser: Controls how LSP completions are inserted.
settings_page.desc.prettier.allowed: Enables or disables formatting with Prettier for a given language.
settings_page.desc.prettier.parser: Forces Prettier integration to use a specific parser name when formatting files with the language.
settings_page.desc.prettier.plugins: Forces Prettier integration to use specific plugins when formatting files with the language.
settings_page.desc.prettier.options: Default Prettier options, in the format as in package.json section for Prettier.
settings_page.desc.prettier.show_edit_pr: Controls whether edit predictions are shown immediately or manually.
settings_page.desc.prettier.disable_in_l: Controls whether edit predictions are shown in the given language scopes.
settings_page.desc.claude_code_plugin_in: Register the plugin that forwards Claude Code task notifications to the Dokkaebi editor by writing to ~/.claude/settings.json.
settings_page.desc.claude_code_task_aler: Show workspace notifications for Claude Code task completion, idle prompts, and permission requests sent by the plugin.
Show the toast popup in the bottom-right corner when a Claude Code task completes. When off, the terminal tab dot and group badge remain but the toast is suppressed.
claude_code.notify.stop.title: Claude Code
claude_code.notify.idle.title: Claude Code
claude_code.notify.permission.title: Claude Code
claude_code.notify.permission.label: Permission request
claude_code.notify.permission.default_bo: Permission required to use a tool.
settings_page.desc.toast_display_seconds: Time before the task completion / idle prompt toast auto-dismisses, in seconds. Range 5–300, default 5. Permission request toasts are not affected.
settings_page.item.enable_keep_preview_o: Enable Keep Preview On Code Navigation
settings_page.item.enable_preview_file_f: Enable Preview File From Code Navigation
settings_page.item.enable_preview_multib: Enable Preview Multibuffer From Code Navigation
settings_page.item.skip_focus_for_active: Skip Focus For Active In Search
settings_page.desc.wallpaper.enable_wall: Display a background image in the workspace.
settings_page.desc.wallpaper.object_fit: How the background image is sized to fit the workspace.
settings_page.desc.wallpaper.background_: Opacity of editor and terminal backgrounds when wallpaper is enabled. Lower values make the wallpaper more visible.
settings_page.desc.git_panel.starts_open: Whether the Git panel should open on startup.
settings_page.desc.keybindings.edit_keyb: Customize keybindings in the keymap editor.
settings_page.desc.agent_configuration.t: Set up regex patterns to auto-allow, auto-deny, or always request confirmation, for specific tool inputs.
settings_page.desc.edit_predictions.conf: Set up different edit prediction providers in complement to the built-in Zeta model.
settings_page.desc.workspace_panel.defau: Default width of the workspace panel in pixels.

language_tools.key_context.title: Keyboard Context
language_tools.key_context.description: This view lets you determine the current context stack for creating custom key bindings in the app. When a keyboard shortcut is triggered, it also shows all the possible contexts it could have triggered in, and which one matched.

copilot_ui.sign_in.description: To use the agent with GitHub Copilot, you need to be logged in to GitHub. Note that your GitHub account must have an active Copilot Chat subscription.
